core_mechanism: coherent interference between a narrow resonant amplitude and a non-resonant background amplitude in a spectral distribution, where the resonance-continuum cross-term dominates the observable line shape (a Fano-type resonance-background interference)
brief_reason: The abstract's central result is that in ttbar invariant-mass spectra the interference of the new-scalar amplitude with the standard (background) amplitude dominates over the narrow-width (pure-resonance) contribution, so the signature lives in the interference cross-term rather than an isolated peak.
